statement: Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) holds 8 Althingi seats in session 157 and is a coalition partner in the current government alongside Samfylkingin and Viðreisn. The party was founded in 2016 by Inga Sæland and is generally EU-sceptic, though it agreed to the coalition's referendum commitment. Current MPs: Inga Sæland (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi suður), Eyjólfur Ármannsson (Norðvesturkjördæmi), Ragnar Þór Ingólfsson (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi norður), Lilja Rafney Magnúsdóttir (Norðvesturkjördæmi), Kolbrún Áslaugar Baldursdóttir (Reykjavíkurkjördæmi suður), Sigurjón Þórðarson (Norðausturkjördæmi), Sigurður Helgi Pálmason (Suðurkjördæmi), Ásthildur Lóa Þórsdóttir (Suðurkjördæmi). Three Flokkur fólksins ministers serve in the cabinet: Eyjólfur Ármannsson (innviðaráðherra), Inga Sæland (félags- og húsnæðismálaráðherra), and Guðmundur Ingi Kristinsson (mennta- og barnamálaráðherra, outside minister).
caveats: Flokkur fólksins's position creates an unusual dynamic: a governing party whose base largely opposes the referendum's pro-EU outcome. Cabinet reshuffles have occurred within the party's ministerial portfolio.

related_entries.statement: The Althingi (Icelandic parliament) has 63 seats. As of session 157, 57 seats are held by sitting MPs. The party breakdown is: Sjálfstæðisflokkur (Independence Party) 13, Samfylkingin (Social Democrats) 13, Viðreisn (Reform Party) 11, Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party) 8, Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) 8, Framsóknarflokkur (Progressive Party) 4. The remaining 6 seats are vacant or held by alternates (varamenn).

The current Icelandic government is a coalition of Samfylkingin (Social Democrats, 13 seats), Viðreisn (Reform Party, 11 seats), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party, 8 seats), holding 32 of 63 Althingi seats. The Prime Minister is Kristrún Frostadóttir (Samfylkingin). This is the first government coalition where both explicitly pro-EU parties (Samfylkingin and Viðreisn) serve together, which has been cited as a factor in advancing the EU referendum.

Sjálfstæðisflokkurinn (Independence Party), Framsóknarflokkurinn (Progressive Party), Miðflokkurinn (Centre Party), and Flokkur fólksins (People's Party) oppose EU membership. The Independence Party has historically been the strongest opponent, citing sovereignty and fisheries concerns. The Progressive Party, traditionally representing rural interests, opposes membership primarily over agricultural and fisheries policy. These four parties collectively held approximately 33–35 of 63 Althingi seats after the 2024 elections.

citations.report_title: 25. þáttur Silfursins: Stefnt að kosningu um ESB í sumar

claims.claim_text: Flokkur fólksins hefur alltaf verið hlynntur beinu lýðræði og Inga Sæland hefur talað fyrir þjóðaratkvæðagreiðslum alveg frá stofnun flokksins.
Inga Sæland var ekki í þættinum vegna þess að hún var erlendis í embættiserindum.
